observations.note: SKILL.md references Customer Discovery workflow but does not explicitly list references/customer-discovery-workflow.md in its reference files section like it does for go-to-market-framework.md and channel-selection-matrix.md. Workflow is discoverable but reference loading pattern is inconsistent.

All 6 workflow phases appear in correct sequential order and map cleanly to the 5 acceptance criteria. Phase 3 (Conduct Interviews) has no dedicated AC but serves as the data collection bridge between loading and synthesis.

All cross-references are consistent: EPIC-074 (optional dependency), EPIC-075 (parent epic), business plan target, state file path using project-relative tmp/ pattern, and confidence score formula (validated/total ratio).
All 4 business rules (BR-001 through BR-004) and NFR-001 are present in both story specification and workflow implementation with consistent semantics.

coverage.component_interactions: 100% (4/4 boundaries: SKILL.md, workflow, story, state file)

recommendations: Add explicit reference loading entry for customer-discovery-workflow.md in SKILL.md On-Demand Reference Loading section
